content:
# Semantic Sub-Splitting Architecture -- Design Decision (2026-03-29)

## What
Add semantic sub-splitting for oversized Markdown heading-section chunks using the Max-Min algorithm with real embeddings. V1 ships as a post-ingest pass (Approach C). Architecture supports swapping to in-pipeline (Approach A) later via SimilaritySource trait abstraction.

## Core Abstraction
SimilaritySource trait separates similarity measurement from the Max-Min splitting algorithm. V1 uses EmbeddingSimilarity (pre-computed embeddings from InferenceEngine). V2 (future) can use InlineSimilarity (wraps InferenceEngine + block_in_place for sync pipeline context).

## V1 Flow (Approach C)
1. pipeline.process_batch() produces ProcessedChunks + ChunkRelations
2. Convert to KnowledgeEntries, embed, store, wire relations (on ORIGINAL chunks)
3. semantic_subsplit() identifies oversized heading_section chunks (> max_tokens threshold)
4. Split into sentences (code blocks/tables atomic, paragraph-first then sentence-level)
5. Embed sentences via engine.embed_batch()
6. Run Max-Min grouping with EmbeddingSimilarity
7. Post-grouping budget check: groups > max_tokens get secondary sentence-boundary split
8. Create new KnowledgeEntries for sub-chunks, embed, store
9. Wire "split_from" graph edges to original entry
10. Mark original entry as superseded

## Key Design Decisions
1. Wire relations BEFORE sub-splitting. Original chunks get full relation wiring. Sub-chunks get "split_from" edges to parent. This avoids breaking the source_index mapping in wire_pipeline_relations.
2. Threshold defaults to max_tokens (512). Only split chunks that are already oversized. Avoids fragmenting well-sized chunks.
3. Sentence segmentation is Markdown-structure-aware: fenced code blocks, tables, HTML blocks are atomic units. Split prose at paragraph boundaries first, then sentence boundaries.
4. Post-grouping token budget enforcement. Max-Min can produce groups > max_tokens. Secondary split at sentence boundaries within the group.
5. Upper bound on sentences per chunk: bail to naive split if > 50 sentences (N^2 similarity matrix).
6. Config: [chunking] semantic_splitting = true/false (default false), with threshold = max_tokens.
7. Re-ingestion: requires --fresh to get clean results. Enabling the feature and running normal ingest supersedes originals.
8. V2 block_on() hazard: must use tokio::task::block_in_place, NOT Runtime::block_on() (panics inside tokio worker).

## Review Findings
- PM: Must define eval plan with MRR@10 baseline before declaring V1 success. Must document re-ingestion story.
- SWE: Relation wiring breaks if chunks are replaced in the processed array. Resolved by wiring first, sub-splitting second.
- SWE: parent_chunk_id: generate synthetic UUID for parent. Sub-chunks reference it. Original entry superseded.
- SWE: ProcessingInfo needs was_semantic_split field for observability.
- Both: Sentence splitting quality is critical. Naive regex on Markdown fails on code blocks, tables, URLs.

## Files
- NEW: crates/corvia-kernel/src/semantic_split.rs (Max-Min algorithm + SimilaritySource trait)
- MODIFIED: crates/corvia-kernel/src/ingest.rs (V1 integration)
- MODIFIED: crates/corvia-common/src/config.rs (ChunkingConfig additions)
- MODIFIED: crates/corvia-kernel/src/chunking_strategy.rs (ProcessingInfo.was_semantic_split)
